title: nymphaea odorata subsp. tuberosa
intro: Nymphaea odorata subsp. tuberosa, also known as the American water lily or fragrant water lily, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the Nymphaea genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and treating respiratory issues, among others. The herbal preparations of Nymphaea odorata subsp. tuberosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
